sentences.base_language: This is a story about Jeff and Bean
sentences.target_language: 这是一个关于Jeff和Bean的故事
sentences.romanized: zhè shì yī gè guānyú Jeff hé Bean de gùshi
sentences.literal: This is one story about Jeff and Bean.

sentences.base_language: One day Bean asked Jeff, "Can I eat chocolate"
sentences.target_language: 有一天Bean问Jeff："我可以吃巧克力吗？"
sentences.romanized: yǒu yī tiān Bean wèn Jeff: "wǒ kěyǐ chī qiǎokèlì ma?"
sentences.literal: Have one day Bean asked Jeff: "I can eat chocolate [question]?"

sentences.base_language: Jeff said, "no, Chocolate is not good for dogs"
sentences.target_language: Jeff说："不行，巧克力对狗不好。"
sentences.romanized: Jeff shuō: "bù xíng, qiǎokèlì duì gǒu bù hǎo."
sentences.literal: Jeff said: "Not okay, chocolate toward dogs not good."
sentences.grammarNote: 对 (duì) means 'toward' or 'for' in the sense of impact: 对狗不好 = 'not good for dogs'. 不行 is a strong 'no' meaning 'not okay/not allowed'.

sentences.base_language: Bean got mad and said, "Why can you eat chocolate but I can't"
sentences.target_language: Bean生气了说："为什么你可以吃巧克力，我不可以？"
sentences.romanized: Bean shēngqì le shuō: "wèishénme nǐ kěyǐ chī qiǎokèlì, wǒ bù kěyǐ?"
sentences.literal: Bean angry [particle] said: "Why you can eat chocolate, I cannot?"

sentences.base_language: Jeff said, "CHOCOLATE ISN'T GOOD FOR DOGS"
sentences.romanized: Jeff shuō: "qiǎokèlì duì gǒu bù hǎo!"
sentences.literal: Jeff said: "Chocolate toward dogs not good!"

sentences.base_language: Bean waited for Jeff to get distracted

sentences.romanized: Bean chī le yī kuài qiǎokèlì
sentences.grammarNote: 块 (kuài) is the measure word for pieces/chunks of things like chocolate, soap, bread.

sentences.base_language: Jeff asked Bean, "where did the chocolate go?"
sentences.romanized: Jeff wèn Bean: "qiǎokèlì qù nǎr le?"

sentences.base_language: Jeff knew that Bean ate the chocolate
sentences.romanized: Jeff zhīdào Bean chī le qiǎokèlì

sentences.base_language: Jeff said, "You are sick because you ate chocolate."
sentences.target_language: Jeff说："你生病是因为你吃了巧克力。"
sentences.romanized: Jeff shuō: "nǐ shēngbìng shì yīnwèi nǐ chī le qiǎokèlì."
sentences.literal: Jeff said: "You sick is because you ate [particle] chocolate."

sentences.base_language: Jeff took bean to the doctor
sentences.romanized: Jeff dài Bean qù kàn yīshēng
sentences.literal: Jeff brought Bean go see doctor.

sentences.base_language: The doctor said, "Bean ate chocolate and he is sick"
sentences.target_language: 医生说："Bean吃了巧克力，所以他病了。"
sentences.romanized: yīshēng shuō: "Bean chī le qiǎokèlì, suǒyǐ tā bìng le."
sentences.literal: Doctor said: "Bean ate [particle] chocolate, therefore he sick [particle]."

sentences.base_language: Bean is such a little scamp
sentences.romanized: Bean zhēn shì gè xiǎo táoqì
sentences.literal: Bean really is [measure word] little rascal.
